sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Larry, has been experiencing chronic back pain for approximately two years, which he describes as a sharp stabbing pain that radiates down to his right leg, specifically to his big toe.
He also reports numbness and tingling in his right leg, especially to his foot, and difficulty getting comfortable, particularly when sitting.
He denies any loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area and no weakness.
He has tried physical therapy and strong pain medications in the past, but with temporary relief only.
He has gained weight due to reduced activity and has been unable to continue playing golf.
Past medical history:
The patient denies any family members with spine conditions and has a history of smoking, but quit 20 years ago.
Review of systems:
The patient reports numbness and tingling in his right leg, difficulty getting comfortable, especially when sitting, and pain radiating to his right leg and big toe.
Current medications:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: [NOT MENTIONED]
Pulse rate: [NOT MENTIONED]
Oxygen saturation: [NOT MENTIONED]
Temperature: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The patient has no overlying erythema or ecchymosis on the musculoskeletal back exam.
There is midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The supine straight leg test is positive.
On neurological examination, there is decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
Patellar and Achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results:
The MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Lumbar back pain; L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy; Radiculopathy.
Plan:
I prescribed a spinal injection with a corticosteroid to help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
The corticosteroid will be injected into the lumbar spine under fluoroscopy.
The patient will be monitored for response to the injection, and the interval between injections will be determined based on individual response.
The patient will not have to do anything long-term, but rather will be monitored for response and potential need for further injections.
Follow-up:
The patient will be scheduled for a follow-up appointment to assess response to the injection and determine the need for further treatment.
